sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Left knee pain.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports occasional left knee pain, occurring less than once daily when walking, often described as giving out and leading to a perceived fall, which he typically catches himself. He frequently holds grocery carts for assistance, finding this helpful. The pain is intermittent and resolves quickly. He denies any history of trauma or injury. He has not taken any medication for the pain. He is planning a trip to Columbus, Georgia, in the near future, including a Lions Club function with planned walking and line dancing, which he anticipates may be limited due to the knee. He reports no significant interference with his daily activities.
Findings
Vital signs are stable: temperature 98.2°F, pulse 72, respirations 16, blood pressure 122/78. Blood sugars have been running slightly higher, approximately 130-130 in the morning. Medications include farxiga, amlodipine, lisinopril, and hydrochlorothiazide, taken regularly. A physical examination reveals no ecchymosis or edema, no effusion, and no pain to palpation of the left medial knee. Range of motion is full and symmetric. Negative varus and valgus tests. A left knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality. A slight two over six systolic ejection murmur is appreciated at the left base.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient's left knee pain is most likely due to osteoarthritis.
Plan
Prescribe meloxicam 15 mg daily. Order physical therapy to strengthen surrounding muscles and prevent further problems. Order an echocardiogram to evaluate the murmur. Order a hemoglobin A1c to assess diabetes management. Schedule follow-up in four weeks or sooner if symptoms persist.
